differentials.name: Papillitis: ← acute pancreatitis, acute cholangitis, passage of biliary stone, periampullary diverticulum, parasite, infection
differentials.raw: Papillitis: ← acute pancreatitis, acute cholangitis, passage of biliary stone, periampullary diverticulum, parasite, infection

context: Prevalence:   1.5% (most common cause) Associated with:  pancreas divisum, history of pancreatitis √  prestenotic dilatation of CBD √  increase in pancreatic duct diameter (83%) √  long smooth narrowing / beak ← fibrotic stenosis √  prolonged bile-to-bowel transit time > 45 minutes on Tc-IDA scintigraphy √  papillary size < 12.3 mm = the only independently reliable variable for differentiating benign from malignant causes N.B.:     for suspected ampullary stenosis employ secretin stimulation test with imaging 4-10 min post injection Bulging Papilla Signs of malignant ampullary obstruction: √
